growth_records.strain: 10403S-GFP (target strain), in direct contact with LLS+ producer strain (F2365 pHELP background)
growth_records.cite: Meza-Torres et al. Listeriolysin S: A bacteriocin from Listeria monocytogenes that induces membrane permeabilization in a contact-dependent manner. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2108155118. [PMC8501752]
growth_records.snippet: the k constant for target cells in direct contact with the LLS+ strain was 0.0097 min−1, with an average doubling time of 110 min.
growth_records.notes: Growth (size-increase) rate constant k directly reported as 0.0097 min^-1 from exponential-curve fitting of single-cell size time-lapse data; doubling time (110 min = 1.83 h) also directly reported. growth_rate_per_h is a unit conversion of the reported k: 0.0097 min^-1 x 60 = 0.582 h^-1 (no formula assumption beyond unit conversion; note reported doubling time of 110 min does not exactly equal ln2/k in h^-1, suggesting k is an empirical fitted size-growth constant rather than a strict ln-based specific growth rate — both reported values are recorded as given).

growth_records.strain: 10403S-GFP (target strain), not in contact with LLS+ or in contact with LLS− (ΔllsA) strain
growth_records.cite: Meza-Torres et al. Listeriolysin S: A bacteriocin from Listeria monocytogenes that induces membrane permeabilization in a contact-dependent manner. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2108155118. [PMC8501752]
growth_records.snippet: the k constant of target cells, either not in contact with producer cells or in contact with LLS−, was 0.02 min−1, with an average doubling time of 40 min, which is expected for rich media–grown Lm.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate constant k directly reported as 0.02 min^-1 (unaffected/control condition); doubling time (40 min = 0.667 h) also directly reported. growth_rate_per_h = 0.02 min^-1 x 60 = 1.2 h^-1 (unit conversion only).
